description: Add .catch(() => {}) guards to sequential cleanup calls in E2E test finally blocks so that a thrown error from one cleanup call (e.g., context.close() on a crashed browser) does not prevent subsequent cleanup of server processes and temp directories.

userStories.title: Guard cleanup calls in helpers.ts setupIsolatedIifeTest
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the setupIsolatedIifeTest cleanup function to guard each async call so that a crash in one cleanup step does not skip the remaining steps.
acceptanceCriteria: The cleanup() function returned by setupIsolatedIifeTest wraps each async cleanup call (client.close, context.close, server.kill, testServer.kill) with .catch(() => {}) so that if any single call throws, the remaining cleanup calls still execute
The existing catch block at lines 444-452 (which already uses .catch(() => {}) guards) is left unchanged

userStories.title: Guard cleanup calls in side-panel-data-flow and side-panel-icons tests
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the manually-managed finally blocks in side-panel-data-flow.e2e.ts and side-panel-icons.e2e.ts to guard cleanup calls so orphaned processes are not left running when Chromium crashes during a test.
acceptanceCriteria: Every finally block in side-panel-data-flow.e2e.ts wraps context.close() with .catch(() => {}) to prevent subsequent server.kill() and testServer.kill() from being skipped
Every finally block in side-panel-icons.e2e.ts wraps context.close() with .catch(() => {}) to prevent subsequent server.kill() from being skipped
All existing cleanup calls (server.kill, testServer.kill, fs.rmSync, cleanupTestConfigDir) remain in the same order

userStories.title: Guard cleanup calls in side-panel-plugin-toggle and side-panel-reload tests
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the manually-managed finally blocks in side-panel-plugin-toggle.e2e.ts and side-panel-reload.e2e.ts to guard cleanup calls so orphaned processes are not left running when Chromium crashes during a test.
acceptanceCriteria: Every finally block in side-panel-plugin-toggle.e2e.ts wraps context.close() with .catch(() => {}) to prevent subsequent server.kill() and testServer.kill() from being skipped
Every finally block in side-panel-reload.e2e.ts wraps context.close() with .catch(() => {}) to prevent subsequent server.kill() from being skipped
All existing cleanup calls remain in the same order
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-plugin-toggle.e2e.ts — 4 finally blocks (around lines 112, 204, 309, 433)
- e2e/side-panel-reload.e2e.ts — 4 finally blocks (around lines 94, 137, 176, 230)

Same pattern as US-002. Each finally block chains:
```
await context.close();  // can throw
await server.kill();
...
```

Fix: `await context.close().catch(() => {});`

Also guard `await mcpClient.close()` where it appears (side-panel-plugin-toggle.e2e.ts lines 205, 310, 434).

Do NOT change the order of cleanup calls or add/remove any cleanup steps.

userStories.title: Guard cleanup calls in remaining side-panel and tab-state-sync tests
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the manually-managed finally blocks in side-panel-live-update.e2e.ts, side-panel-port-change.e2e.ts, onboarding.e2e.ts, and tab-state-sync.e2e.ts to guard cleanup calls so orphaned processes are not left running when Chromium crashes during a test.
acceptanceCriteria: Every finally block in side-panel-live-update.e2e.ts wraps context.close() with .catch(() => {})
Every finally block in side-panel-port-change.e2e.ts wraps context.close() with .catch(() => {})
Every finally block in onboarding.e2e.ts wraps context?.close() with .catch(() => {})
The finally block in the tab-state-sync.e2e.ts server restart test wraps context.close() with .catch(() => {})
All existing cleanup calls remain in the same order
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-live-update.e2e.ts — 2 finally blocks (around lines 95 and 139)
- e2e/side-panel-port-change.e2e.ts — 1 finally block (around line 75+)
- e2e/onboarding.e2e.ts — 4 finally blocks (around lines 71, 119, 155, 204). Note: these use optional chaining `await context?.close()` — change to `await context?.close().catch(() => {})`
- e2e/tab-state-sync.e2e.ts — 1 finally block in the server restart test (around line 416). Pattern: `await context.close();`

Same fix pattern as previous stories: wrap `context.close()` (or `context?.close()`) with `.catch(() => {})`.

For onboarding.e2e.ts, note the optional chaining: `await context?.close().catch(() => {})` is valid JS — if context is undefined, the expression short-circuits to undefined and .catch is never called.

Do NOT change the order of cleanup calls or add/remove any cleanup steps. The fix is purely adding .catch(() => {}) guards.
